mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 240.0, further increases in the federal funds rate trigger profit margin compression and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of cases where FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL above 240.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS levels against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any consecutive 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% quarter-over-quarter signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates robust economic momentum without labor market overheating, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day returns (coefficient insignificant at p > 0.05) across multiple market regimes.
